text:
Kenyan runner Kelvin Kiptum, the marathon world record holder, died in a car crash Sunday night at age 24.

Kiptum was driving the car with his coach,Â Gervais Hakizimana, as a passenger, in the village ofÂ Kaptagat in western Kenya when the car veered off the road and ran into a tree.Â Hakizimana also was killed.Â A woman was also in the car, and she was taken to a hospital with serious injuries,according to theAssociated Press.

Kiptumâs world record ofÂ 2:00.35Â was set in October at the Chicago Marathon. The record officially was ratified byÂ international track federation World Athletics just last week. It was only the third major marathon ofÂ KiptumâsÂ career.

The 24-year-old was a favorite to win gold in the marathon at the 2024 Paris Olympics this summer.

Kiptum was slate to compete at theÂ Rotterdam Marathon in April, which wouldâve been his first race since breaking the world record in Chicago.

title: Marathon World Record Holder Kelvin Kiptum Dies at 24
description: The Kenyan runner was viewed as a favorite for the marathon gold medal at the 2024 Paris Olympics.
